info.title: Configuration Service
info.description: Device/PV registry for Bluesky Remote Architecture (SVC-004). Plans are managed by Experiment Execution Service.

get.description:
Health check: state loaded AND registry DB queryable.

Runs ``SELECT 1`` against the registry store (when DB mode is on)
and returns 503 with the failure detail when it can't. Without
the DB ping, a mount-gone or permissions-revoked store would
still pass health while every CRUD call 500'd.
Readiness check endpoint.
Query available devices from registry

get.description: Return device-state changes after a given audit cursor. Intended for clients (like bluesky-queueserver) that cache device state and need to keep it fresh without re-fetching the full registry. Each device is reported at most once with its current state. If reset_occurred is true, the caller should discard local state and re-fetch /devices-info; likewise if service_epoch differs from a previously-observed value.

parameters.name: since_version

schema.description: Return changes with audit id greater than this value
schema.title: Since Version

parameters.description: Return changes with audit id greater than this value

post.description:
Acquire locks on multiple devices atomically (all-or-nothing).

When a device is locked, all PVs belonging to that device are implicitly
locked. Direct Control checks PV/device status before every write operation
and will refuse to command locked PVs.

Locks are held for the duration of a Bluesky plan (minutes to hours).
EE sends unlock when the plan completes, fails, or is aborted.

409.description: One or more devices already locked

404.description: One or more devices not found

422.description: One or more devices are disabled

post.description:
Release locks on devices. Only the item_id that acquired the lock can
release it. Use force-unlock for admin override.

Emergency endpoint to clear stale locks regardless of ownership.

Use when EE crashes mid-plan and locks are orphaned. Requires ADMIN
role when AuthZ middleware is enabled.

get.description:
Combined availability check: lock state + enabled/disabled.

A device is available only when it is both enabled and unlocked.

Disable a device so it will not be instantiated by remote services

get.description:
Check whether a PV can be commanded (caput).

Resolves PV to its owning device and returns the device's lock and
enabled state. Standalone PVs (not bound to a device) are always
available. This is the primary endpoint Direct Control calls before
every write operation.
Get detailed metadata for specific PV
Look up nested device component by dot-separated path

post.summary: Resolve Dotted Device Addresses to PV Names
post.description: Walk the ophyd / ophyd-async device class for each address and return the underlying EPICS PV. Read-only, best-effort per-item — no halt-on-error. Used by the frontend to translate friendly addresses like 'vortex.mca.rois.roi2.lo_chan' into the PV strings needed by direct-control's batch caput.

DeviceAuditEntry.title: DeviceAuditEntry
DeviceAuditEntry.description: Entry in the device audit log (append-only change history).

DeviceCRUDResponse.title: DeviceCRUDResponse
DeviceCRUDResponse.description: Response model for device CRUD operations.

version.title: Version
version.description: Audit log id of the change that produced this state

DeviceChangeEntry.title: DeviceChangeEntry
DeviceChangeEntry.description:
One device's current state, reported in a delta response.

``op`` is "upsert" if the device currently exists (create or update), or
"delete" if it no longer exists. For "upsert", ``metadata`` and ``spec``
reflect the current state; for "delete" both are null.

current_version.title: Current Version
current_version.description: Latest audit log id at query time

reset_occurred.title: Reset Occurred
reset_occurred.description: True if a registry-wide reset happened in the range

DeviceChangesResponse.title: DeviceChangesResponse
DeviceChangesResponse.description:
Delta response: every device whose state changed after ``since_version``.

If ``reset_occurred`` is true, a registry-wide reset happened in the
requested range and the caller should discard its local state and
re-fetch the full registry instead of applying ``changes`` incrementally.
``service_epoch`` is a stable identifier that changes only on manual
re-seed or DB wipe; if it differs from the value the caller saw last,
treat the cursor as invalid.

DeviceCreateRequest.title: DeviceCreateRequest
DeviceCreateRequest.description: Request model for creating a runtime device.

example.reason: EE crashed during rel_scan, clearing stale locks

active.title: Active
active.description: Whether this device should be instantiated

DeviceInstantiationSpec.title: DeviceInstantiationSpec
DeviceInstantiationSpec.description:
Device instantiation specification for remote device creation.

This model contains all information needed to recreate a device instance
in another service (e.g., Experiment Execution Service). By providing
the class path and constructor arguments, remote services can dynamically
import and instantiate identical device objects.

This enables Configuration Service to be the single source of truth for
device definitions, ensuring PV names and configurations are consistent
across all services.

active.title: Active
active.description: Whether this device should be instantiated

DeviceInstantiationSpecUpdate.title: DeviceInstantiationSpecUpdate
DeviceInstantiationSpecUpdate.description:
Partial of DeviceInstantiationSpec for PATCH/PUT updates.

Every field is Optional with default None so callers can send only
the fields they want to change. Pair with ``model_dump(exclude_unset=True)``
to distinguish "not sent" from "sent as None". Field set must mirror
DeviceInstantiationSpec — enforced by test_partial_models_field_parity.

DeviceLabel.title: DeviceLabel
DeviceLabel.description:
Device classification derived from ophyd/ophyd-async class hierarchy.

Each value maps directly to a concrete base class in ophyd or ophyd-async:
  MOTOR     — ophyd.EpicsMotor, ophyd_async.epics.motor.Motor
  DETECTOR  — ophyd.areadetector.DetectorBase, ophyd_async.core.StandardDetector
  SIGNAL    — ophyd.Signal/EpicsSignal, ophyd_async.core.Signal
  FLYER     — ophyd.FlyerInterface, ophyd_async.core.StandardFlyer
  READABLE  — ophyd_async.core.StandardReadable (readable but not motor/detector)
  DEVICE    — ophyd.Device, ophyd_async.core.Device (generic base)

DeviceMetadata.title: DeviceMetadata
DeviceMetadata.description:
Device metadata model.

Represents device information loaded from profile collections.
Maps to ProvidesDeviceRegistry.get_device() return type.

Compatible with ophyd/ophyd-async device introspection and profile collection formats.

module.title: Module

parent.title: Parent

DeviceMetadataUpdate.title: DeviceMetadataUpdate
DeviceMetadataUpdate.description:
Partial of DeviceMetadata for PATCH/PUT updates.

Every field is Optional with default None so callers can send only
the fields they want to change. Pair with ``model_dump(exclude_unset=True)``
to distinguish "not sent" from "sent as None". Field set must mirror
DeviceMetadata — enforced by test_partial_models_field_parity.

locked_by_item.title: Locked By Item

instantiation_spec.description: Partial instantiation spec — only included fields are updated

DeviceUpdateRequest.title: DeviceUpdateRequest
DeviceUpdateRequest.description:
Request model for updating a device.

Supports field-level partial updates: only the fields you include
in ``metadata`` or ``instantiation_spec`` are changed.  Omitted fields
keep their current values.

available.title: Available

device_enabled.title: Device Enabled

locked_by_plan.title: Locked By Plan

PVStatusResponse.title: PVStatusResponse
PVStatusResponse.description: PV availability check (resolves PV to owning device lock state).

PathResolveRequest.title: PathResolveRequest
PathResolveRequest.description:
Batch request to resolve dotted device addresses to PV names.

Each address is one of:
- ``"<device>"`` — top-level happi entry (e.g. a standalone EpicsSignal);
  resolves to the device's prefix.
- ``"<device>.<attr>.<attr>..."`` — walks the device class's Component
  tree (Component / DynamicDeviceComponent / FormattedComponent).

Resolution is purely static: configuration_service imports the device
class and inspects its Components without instantiating the device and
without opening any EPICS connections. ``FormattedComponent`` suffixes
with ``{}`` placeholders return ``needs_enrichment`` since they require
a live parent to evaluate.

PathResolveResponse.title: PathResolveResponse
PathResolveResponse.description:
Aggregate response for a batch resolve.

Always 200 — per-address outcomes are in the rows. Resolution is
read-only with no state change, so "best effort with per-item errors"
is the right semantic; unlike batch caput there's no halt-on-failure.

outcome.title: Outcome
outcome.description: One of: resolved | device_not_found | import_failed | no_such_attr | needs_enrichment

PathResolveResultItem.title: PathResolveResultItem
PathResolveResultItem.description:
One row in the batch response.

``ok=true`` iff ``outcome == 'resolved'`` and ``pv_name`` is set.
Other outcomes (``device_not_found``, ``import_failed``,
``no_such_attr``, ``needs_enrichment``) carry their reason in
``message`` so the frontend can show the operator why a particular
address couldn't be resolved.

StandalonePV.title: StandalonePV
StandalonePV.description: A standalone PV not associated with any ophyd device.

StandalonePVCRUDResponse.title: StandalonePVCRUDResponse
StandalonePVCRUDResponse.description: Response model for standalone PV CRUD operations.

StandalonePVCreateRequest.title: StandalonePVCreateRequest
StandalonePVCreateRequest.description: Request model for registering a standalone PV.

StandalonePVUpdateRequest.title: StandalonePVUpdateRequest
StandalonePVUpdateRequest.description:
Request model for updating a standalone PV.

All fields optional.  Only fields included in the request body are
applied; omitted fields keep their current values.
